name: Alert State
description: An AI perception state machine that moves agents between unaware, suspicious, searching, alerted, and combat-ready behavior based on perception events.
design_notes: Alert state must define how perception_event_id, suspicion score, last_known_player_position, line-of-sight grace, and transition hysteresis interact. The system should explain why an AI changed state through debug_reason_trace and avoid instant binary detection unless the game intentionally wants harsh stealth failure.
design_purpose: Create readable escalation between hidden play, investigation, search, and combat.
Give designers control over how sound, sight, damage, and scripted events compete for AI priority.
Support stealth recovery by letting suspicion decay or move into search rather than permanent combat.
player_fantasy: The player feels watched but not instantly doomed, reading enemy suspicion and exploiting recovery windows.

parameters.name: alert_state_id
parameters.description: Current AI awareness state for the agent.

parameters.name: suspicion_threshold_points
parameters.description: Suspicion score required before escalating to a higher alert state.

parameters.name: suspicion_decay_points_per_second
parameters.description: Decay applied when no reinforcing perception events arrive.

parameters.name: line_of_sight_grace_ms
parameters.description: Grace interval before losing sight clears the current visual confirmation.

parameters.name: search_timeout_ms
parameters.description: Search interval before returning toward a lower awareness state.

parameters.name: alert_hysteresis_margin_points
parameters.description: Margin that prevents rapid state flipping near thresholds.

edge_cases: A guard hears a noise and sees the player in the same decision tick, requiring deterministic perception priority.
The player breaks line of sight during the grace interval and leaves a last_known_player_position for search.
Checkpoint restore should not resurrect a combat alert unless that state was intentionally saved.
Multiple AI agents sharing alert data need squad_id rules for propagation and cooldown.
common_bugs: Suspicion oscillates every tick because threshold and decay have no hysteresis margin.
AI enters combat without storing the perception_event_id that caused the transition.
Awareness UI shows suspicious while behavior tree already selected attack actions.
Save/load restores alert_state_id but not last_known_player_position or search timer.
balancing_notes: Long grace windows make stealth more forgiving; short windows favor strict line-of-sight play.
Group alert propagation can make a single mistake cascade, so squads need distance or communication limits.
Suspicion decay should be tuned with patrol density and hiding opportunities, not in isolation.
accessibility_notes: Represent alert state with shape, text, or meter changes in addition to color.
Provide non-audio cues for suspicion gain caused by sound events so deaf or low-volume players can read the system.

implementation_notes.unity: Store alert_state_id, suspicion points, last_known_player_position, and debug_reason_trace on an AI blackboard component. Perception events should enqueue with priority, then one deterministic decision step updates state before behavior tree or animation transitions run.
implementation_notes.godot: Use an AI state node or Resource-backed blackboard with suspicion score and perception_event_id fields. Update transitions in _physics_process or a fixed AI tick, and expose debug_reason_trace in editor overlays for patrol testing.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use AI Perception plus Blackboard keys for alert_state_id, target_id, and last known position. Decorators should use hysteresis thresholds, while replicated or networked AI keeps authority on the server.
implementation_notes.web: Represent alert transitions as deterministic data in the AI update loop. Sort perception events by priority and stable id, update suspicion and search timers, then render awareness UI from committed state.

relationships.reason: Alert state needs perception events from line-of-sight checks to escalate suspicion or combat.
Noise events can raise suspicion or alert state even without visual contact.
Losing direct detection can feed last-known-position search behavior.

example_games.title: Metal Gear Solid V
example_games.note: Uses escalating enemy awareness and search behavior in stealth encounters.

example_games.title: Dishonored
example_games.note: Uses guard alertness states to move from suspicion to combat.
